items.title: EDPB meets with EU Commissioner McGrath and adopts common data breach notification template
items.summary: Brussels, 10 June – During its latest plenary, the EDPB met with Michael McGrath, Commissioner for Democracy, Justice, the Rule of Law and Consumer Protection. In addition, the Board has adopted a common data breach notification template . The Board held a meeting with Commission…

items.title: Sound Practices for Responsible Adoption of Artificial Intelligence (AI): Consultation report
items.summary: The FSB has developed sound practices to help all types of financial institutions navigate benefits and risks responsibly as they adopt AI.

items.title: FSB consults on sound practices for the responsible adoption of artificial intelligence (AI)
items.summary: FSB seeks feedback on proposed sound practices for financial institutions to adopt, use, and innovate with AI in a responsible manner.

items.title: NIST Expands Its Library of ‘Chemical Fingerprints’ to Identify Unknown Substances
items.summary: Researchers and manufacturers rely on the library to identify unknown compounds in food, drugs, cosmetics, the environment, body fluids, forensic evidence and even space rocks.

items.title: NIST Mathematical Proof Supports Transition to a Continuous-Monitor-and-Update Security Model for AI Systems
items.summary: The proof extends to AI the logic used by famed mathematician Kurt Gödel, whose incompleteness theorems have had a profound effect on math for nearly a century.

items.summary: FSB hosts a symposium to support members’ efforts in supervisory and regulatory modernisation.

items.summary: A NIST-led team has created a new AI model that can identify safe evacuation routes in a single-story floor plan during a fire, with a multilevel version in the works.

items.title: Any Color You Like: NIST Scientists Create ‘Any Wavelength’ Lasers in Tiny Circuits for Light
items.summary: NIST scientists and collaborators have pioneered a way to make integrated circuits for light by depositing complex patterns of specialized materials onto silicon wafers.

items.title: NIST Researchers Develop Photonic Chip Packaging That Can Withstand Extreme Environments
items.summary: The advance could allow these technologies to operate in deep-space probes, inside nuclear reactors, in ultrahigh vacuum systems, and at temperatures both near absolute zero and in scorchingly hot industrial settings.
